title: Xia: Legends of a Drift System - Part Two - The Anxious First Days of a Greenhorn Space Captain
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FSFC8O9LxRU

Daniel (@dungeondive) — now Captain D of the tier-one Ghost Stalker — uses Part Two to map out the many roads to victory in Xia: Legends of a Drift System, showing newcomers just how open-world this sandbox is. He outfits his ship (an engine, a shield, an extended cargo hold) deliberately weapon-free for a peaceful, trade-and-explore start, and walks the score track he's set to a short 10-fame game.

The tour of fame sources is the heart of it: trading two exploration tokens for fame, completing bounty or "good" missions, blowing up NPC ships (worth their tier in fame), an economic victory via the expansion's supply-and-demand economy board, claiming titles (like "the Economist"), buying new ships and outright fame, salvaging relics from dead worlds for the Kiln to sift, activating teleport gates, surviving events, and the catch-all — rolling a natural 20 for "pure luck." He details the four NPCs (Scoundrel, Enforcer, Merchant, Kiln) and their two-behaviour AI cards that shape both the mechanics and the emerging story, plus easy/hard stat sides.

His repeated point: Xia never lacks for things to do — which is exactly why he and so many players love it.

----

With this many paths to victory, which would you chase first in Xia — the trade economy, the missions, or pure exploration?
